title: Tarenna microcarpa
intro: Tarenna microcarpa, also known as Tarenna, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, making it a potential treatment for various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna microcarpa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Tarenna include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress.
taxonomy: Tarenna microcarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Tarenna and is native to tropical Asia. The classification of Tarenna microcarpa places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Its classification also groups it within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants.
